title: InCharacter
protocol_summary: Official protocol runs a full questionnaire bundle for each character, converts responses back into benchmark-native choice scores with an evaluator LLM, and computes single-dimension and full-profile alignment against the released labels.
implementation_note: Recovered as an official local task by switching from dimension slices to full questionnaire bundles and restoring evaluator-based answer conversion plus bundle-level accuracy reporting.
summary: The local runnable path now follows the official questionnaire-bundle evaluation flow, including evaluator conversion and bundle-level label alignment reporting.

title: CharacterBench
protocol_summary: Official protocol first generates character responses on the released test set, then converts them into CharacterJudge prompts, and finally scores them with the CharacterJudge evaluator.
implementation_note: Recovered as an official local task by restoring upstream CharacterJudge prompt construction over the released evaluation subsets and normalized score aggregation.
summary: The local runnable path now uses the released CharacterJudge prompt templates across the full CharacterBench evaluation set.

title: SocialBench
protocol_summary: Official protocol formats dialogue-plus-profile prompts per SocialBench category and computes deterministic choice or keyword-based scores across the published sociality dimensions.
implementation_note: Wave start: audited prompt formatting and deterministic scoring against the released SocialBench code path and restored the local task to the official runnable lane.
summary: Prompt construction and scoring have now been re-audited against the upstream release, so SocialBench is restored as an official runnable local benchmark.

title: TimeChara
protocol_summary: Official TimeChara evaluation uses the released test split plus the published spatiotemporal judge prompt to score future, past-absence, past-presence, and past-only timeline consistency.
implementation_note: Recovered as an official local task by wiring the released test split to the benchmark's published spatiotemporal judging prompt and score-composition rules.
summary: The local runnable path now follows the released TimeChara spatiotemporal evaluation contract over the full test split.

title: PersonaFeedback
protocol_summary: The local task keeps the released multiple-choice persona-conditioned response-selection contract and scores exact-match accuracy on the public benchmark items.
implementation_note: Runnable baseline retained while the current wave focuses implementation effort on the planned role-play benchmark set.
summary: Retained in the runnable lane during this cleanup because no concrete protocol mismatch has been identified yet between the local task and the released benchmark contract.

title: PersonaMem
protocol_summary: The local task keeps the released long-context preference-selection benchmark format and evaluates exact-match accuracy over the 32k-context split.
implementation_note: Runnable baseline retained while the current wave focuses implementation effort on the planned role-play benchmark set.
summary: Retained as runnable because the local task still mirrors the released benchmark structure closely enough for this cleanup phase.

title: AlpsBench Task 1 (Extraction)
protocol_summary: Task 1 extracts stable user memories from dialogue; the paper describes memory matching plus structured judging for extraction quality.
implementation_note: The local task preserves the released public validation contract and scorer, but the full benchmark-side judge stack is not public.
summary: Runnable through the public Task 1 release, scored with the official public local metric rather than the unreleased benchmark-side judge.

title: AlpsBench Task 2 (Update)
protocol_summary: Task 2 updates the user's long-term memory from existing memories plus old and new dialogue, with memory matching and structured judging described in the paper.
implementation_note: The local task keeps the released public validation contract and scorer, while the benchmark-side structured judge remains unreleased.
summary: Runnable through the public Task 2 release, using the official public scorer as a proxy for the hidden benchmark-side evaluation.

title: AlpsBench Task 3 (Retrieval)
protocol_summary: Task 3 answers a user query from a candidate memory pool; the benchmark-side evaluation judges whether the model used the correct supporting memory.
implementation_note: The local task follows the released public prediction contract and selected-memory scorer because the benchmark-side usage judge is not fully public.
summary: Runnable through the public Task 3 release, with public selected-memory accuracy standing in for the hidden usage judge.

title: AlpsBench Task 4 (Utilization)
protocol_summary: Task 4 generates personalized responses across five abilities, with benchmark-side scoring handled by ability-specific structured judges.
implementation_note: The local task preserves the released public prediction contract and grounding proxy, because the full Task 4 judge prompts and decision rules are not released.
summary: Runnable through the public Task 4 release, scored with the official public grounding proxy instead of the hidden ability-specific judges.

title: ALPBench
protocol_summary: The local task keeps the released latent-attribute classification contract and computes label-match accuracy on the public benchmark split.
implementation_note: Runnable baseline retained while the current wave focuses implementation effort on the planned role-play benchmark set.
summary: Retained as a runnable local task because no concrete mismatch has yet been identified between the local classification contract and the released benchmark setup.
